bottom_line: S05's spurious fix cycle signals a daemon PID-watchdog false alarm; pre-existing lint errors are noted redundantly in every step report. Both are one-off or low-severity — no critical process breakdowns detected.
coverage_notes: Read S01–S05 reports in full. No raw run logs available (worktree logs at .worktrees/I-00064/ai-dev/ absent — worktree was likely reaped after S05 completed). Fix-cycle prompt at ai-dev/active/I-00064/fix-cycles/I-00064_S05_FIX_cycle1_prompt.md shows a spurious PID-death signal with no actual agent work needed. DB telemetry: full (DB:UP confirmed).

findings.title: S05 fix cycle was spurious — daemon PID-watchdog false alarm
findings.recommendation: Investigate whether the daemon's PID-exit detection fires on normal agent exit vs actual crash. If it fires on clean exit, the fix-cycle trigger is a false-positive engine wasting review cycles.
evidence: ai-dev/active/I-00064/reports/I-00064_S05_CodeReview_Final_report.md — PASS with 0 mandatory fixes confirms nothing was broken

findings.title: Pre-existing TC004 lint error redundantly noted by every implementation step
findings.recommendation: CLAUDE.md addendum: pre-existing lint violations should be labeled `skipped:pre-existing:{file}:{error-code}` in preflight rather than prose-noted in every report.
